# NIST ACVP ML-DSA-65 sigVer Vectors

## Source

**Repository:** [usnistgov/ACVP-Server](https://github.com/usnistgov/ACVP-Server/tree/master/gen-val/json-files/ML-DSA-sigVer-FIPS204)

**Source URL:** `https://github.com/usnistgov/ACVP-Server/tree/master/gen-val/json-files/ML-DSA-sigVer-FIPS204`

## License / Terms

NIST is a US federal government agency. Content produced by NIST staff is generally
in the public domain. See
[NIST copyright and fair-use statement](https://www.nist.gov/open/copyright-fair-use-and-licensing-statements-srd-data-software-and-technical-series-publications).

No vendor-specific copyright is asserted in the ACVP-Server repository.

## Algorithm

- **Algorithm:** ML-DSA-65
- **Standard:** FIPS 204 (Module-Lattice-Based Digital Signature Standard)
- **ACVP revision:** FIPS204
- **Mode:** sigVer (signature verification)
- **vsId:** 42
- **Test group:** 3 (ML-DSA-65, external interface, pure / no pre-hash)
- **Test type:** AFT (Algorithm Functional Test)

## Security level

ML-DSA-65 targets NIST security category 3 (≥ AES-192 collision resistance).

## Encoding assumptions

All hex fields (`pk`, `message`, `context`, `signature`) are lowercase hex strings
**without** a `0x` prefix.

| Field       | Expected byte length | Notes                             |
|-------------|---------------------|-----------------------------------|
| `pk`        | 1952                | ML-DSA-65 public key              |
| `signature` | 3309                | ML-DSA-65 signature               |
| `message`   | variable            | Original message before signing   |
| `context`   | 0–255               | Empty string means no context     |

`testPassed: true` means the vector is a valid (message, pk, signature) triple.
`testPassed: false` means the vector contains a deliberate perturbation expected to
fail verification.

## Vector selection

The ACVP sigVer prompt for vsId 42 contains 180 test cases across 12 groups covering
ML-DSA-44, ML-DSA-65, and ML-DSA-87 parameter sets and multiple interfaces. This
fixture includes a 6-vector subset from **group 3 only**:

- All 3 valid (testPassed: true) vectors from group 3 — tcId 31, 35, 37
- The first 3 invalid (testPassed: false) vectors from group 3 — tcId 32, 33, 34

Group 3 was selected because it uses the **external interface, pure (no pre-hash)**
mode, which is the mode used by `@noble/post-quantum` `ml_dsa65.verify` and by the
`verifyMLDSA65` helper in `scripts/lib/attestation.ts`.

tcId 35 specifically uses an **empty context string**, making it directly exercisable
through the `verifyMLDSA65` wrapper (which does not pass a context, defaulting to
empty).

## Whether vectors are official

Yes. These vectors were generated by and published by NIST as part of the Automated
Cryptographic Validation Protocol (ACVP) test infrastructure. They are used to validate
cryptographic implementations against FIPS 204.

## Noble post-quantum compatibility

`@noble/post-quantum` `ml_dsa65.verify(sig, msg, pk, { context })` passes the
**6 committed vectors** in this fixture (3 valid + 3 invalid group-3 ACVP sigVer
vectors — see "Vector selection" above). This fixture does not include the
group's other 9 invalid vectors, so this is a committed subset, not the full
15/15 group-3 result. `@noble/post-quantum` is independently ACVP-tested
upstream by its maintainer; see
[`@noble/post-quantum`](https://github.com/paulmillr/noble-post-quantum) for
its own conformance claims. See `test/MLDSAConformance.test.ts` for the test
that exercises this fixture.

## Vectors not included

- Groups 1–2 (ML-DSA-44)
- Groups 5–6, 11–12 (ML-DSA-87)
- Group 4 (ML-DSA-65, external, preHash)
- Groups 7–8 (ML-DSA-44 internal), 9–10 (ML-DSA-65 internal)

Pre-hash (group 4) and internal-interface (groups 9–10) vectors are not included here
because `verifyMLDSA65` and the attestation path use the external pure interface only.
Noble does not pass 100% of the preHash/internal ACVP vectors through the standard
`ml_dsa65.verify` call (those interfaces require different API paths).
